name: browser_evaluate
description:
Execute JavaScript in the page for debugging and inspection. RESTRICTIONS: Cannot access document.cookie, localStorage, sessionStorage, or credentials. Use for DOM inspection and debugging only.
WHEN TO USE: Only use browser_evaluate when browser_snapshot cannot detect the elements you need (e.g., custom components without ARIA roles, elements hidden from accessibility tree). Always try browser_snapshot + browser_click/browser_type first.

USAGE RULES:
- Keep scripts SHORT and focused. Do one thing per call: either inspect OR act, not both.
- Always use `return` to return a structured result object (e.g., `return { found: true, text: "..." }`).
- Double-check bracket matching and JS syntax before calling — syntax errors cause silent failures with "Script failed to execute".
- When setting input values, MUST dispatch events: `el.value = 'x'; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true }));`
- Avoid scripts longer than 15 lines. If you need complex logic, split into multiple evaluate calls.

script.description: JavaScript to evaluate in the page context. Use return to return a value. Cannot access cookies, localStorage, or sessionStorage.

viewId.description: Target browser tab ID. If omitted, uses the last interacted tab.
